askNext: What exact meaning of Z.AI is being used here?
What changes operationally if this label is removed from the sentence?
Is this naming a mechanism, a vendor choice, a risk, or a wish?
z-ai.body: Z.AI earns its own page because branded nouns often hide decisions about platform control, pricing, permissions, and operational dependency. This entry keeps the name visible without letting it do the architecture's work.
z-ai.example: "Z.AI came up in the review, which was useful only after the team clarified whether they meant the product, the platform, the model family, or the vendor relationship."
z-ai.misuse: It gets abused when the name is used as a substitute for specifying the exact product surface, model, API, entitlement, pricing constraint, or operational dependency under discussion.
z-ai.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means someone should ask what is being bought, integrated, routed through, depended on, or compared before the logo is allowed to stand in for the architecture.

translations.label: procurement meaning
translations.text: Treat the name as a dependency to inspect, not a badge to admire.

z-ai.warningLabel: A brand-shaped noun is not a technical specification.
z-ai.whyExists: The term matters because discussions around GLM often need to separate the actual technical choice from the brand, distribution channel, contract, or ecosystem wrapped around it.

askNext: Does the promise cover prompts, outputs, logs, attachments, and every relevant product surface?
What operational or abuse-monitoring exceptions still exist?
Is the policy a contractual guarantee, a best-effort setting, or a line in a landing page?
zero-data-retention.body: Most privacy language sounds best at banner height. The truth usually begins one scroll lower.
zero-data-retention.example: "The security team relaxed at zero data retention, then tensed again when the discussion moved from slogan to specifics."
zero-data-retention.misuse: By treating it as a synonym for perfect secrecy. A zero-retention promise usually lives inside a wider reality of transient processing, abuse monitoring, metadata, contracts, and implementation details people discover only after reading the fine print.
zero-data-retention.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means a procurement checkbox with real value, provided someone also asks about logs, sub-processors, retention exceptions, and whether the promise applies across every feature being sold under the same cheerful brand.

translations.label: buyer meaning
translations.text: A line item that can make legal and security conversations somewhat less apocalyptic.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A constrained non-retention policy whose exact safety depends on the surrounding details.

zero-data-retention.warningLabel: Useful promise. Not a substitute for reading the retention details around logs, attachments, and exceptions.
zero-data-retention.whyExists: Because enterprise buyers, regulated industries, and anyone with a functioning sense of self-preservation want stronger assurances about where sensitive inputs end up after an AI request is made.

askNext: What exact meaning of Zero-click search is being used here?
What changes operationally if this label is removed from the sentence?
Is this naming a mechanism, a vendor choice, a risk, or a wish?
zero-click-search.body: Zero-click search gets its own page because the surrounding discussion around AI Overviews keeps needing this sharper distinction. Used well, it turns a vague AI-adjacent gesture into a claim someone can test, budget for, or push back on.
zero-click-search.example: "Once zero-click search entered the discussion, the team finally had a name for the thing everyone had been gesturing at with less precision."
zero-click-search.misuse: It gets abused when people use the phrase as a mood, a shortcut, or a status signal instead of explaining the actual mechanism, incentive, or trade-off they mean.
zero-click-search.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means slowing the conversation down enough to ask whether the label points to a real system property, a commercial incentive, a user behaviour, or just a familiar rhetorical flourish.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A useful nearby term, provided someone explains the mechanism instead of using it as scenery.

zero-click-search.whyExists: It exists because the main term alone is often too blunt. The surrounding conversation needs a way to name the neighbouring idea without pretending it is the same thing.

askNext: Is the task genuinely being done from instructions alone, or is other hidden scaffolding doing the work?
What examples, schemas, or retrieval would reduce failure without much extra cost?
Are we calling this zero-shot because it is elegant, or because we have not designed the workflow yet?
zero-shot.body: Zero-shot is attractive because it feels like generality. It is also attractive because it postpones the adult work of specifying the task properly.
zero-shot.example: "They celebrated the zero-shot result right up to the point where the real workflow arrived with edge cases and an invoice."
zero-shot.misuse: By treating zero-shot success as proof of broad understanding rather than a description of prompt setup. Many so-called zero-shot wins rely on hidden context, careful wording, or tasks that were never especially demanding.
zero-shot.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means the cheapest first attempt. If the task is routine and the instructions are clear, zero-shot can be enough. If the task is messy, high-stakes, or structurally ambiguous, it often becomes a polite way to under-specify the work.

translations.label: demo meaning
translations.text: A neat way to show breadth before anyone asks what the production version needs.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A task attempted without task-specific examples, for better or worse.

zero-shot.whyExists: It is useful to distinguish between tasks a model can handle from instructions alone and tasks that need demonstrations, schemas, retrieval, or tighter scaffolding.
